task_context.actionable_question: Am I getting better or worse over time?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Identify trend direction, assessments below pass/target thresholds, and the concrete recommended_action for the weakest recent assessment.

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: Your performance has improved significantly over the assessments, moving from a score of 0 in Assessment 1 to 55 in Assessment 3. This shows a positive trend in your understanding and skills over time.

Insights: Significant Improvement: You increased your score from 0 in Assessment 1 to 55 in Assessment 3, which is a delta of 55. This indicates a strong upward trend in your performance. | Initial Struggles: In Assessment 1, you scored 0, which is below both the pass and target thresholds. This indicates a need for urgent support at that time. | Consistent Target Practice Needed: Despite passing in Assessments 2 and 3, your scores remain below the target threshold of 70, indicating that further practice is necessary to reach your full potential.

Educational implications: Your improvement suggests that with the right support and practice, you can enhance your understanding of the material. | The need for targeted practice indicates that while you are passing, there is still room for growth to reach your target performance.

Recommendations: high - Review Assessment 1 with tutor support to focus on missed core concepts. - This will help solidify your understanding and prevent similar struggles in future assessments. | medium - Engage in targeted practice on topics related to Assessments 2 and 3. - This will help you move from pass-level to target-level performance.

explanation.summary: Your performance has improved significantly over the assessments, moving from a score of 0 in Assessment 1 to 55 in Assessment 3. This shows a positive trend in your understanding and skills over time.

insights.title: Significant Improvement
insights.description: You increased your score from 0 in Assessment 1 to 55 in Assessment 3, which is a delta of 55. This indicates a strong upward trend in your performance.

insights.title: Initial Struggles
insights.description: In Assessment 1, you scored 0, which is below both the pass and target thresholds. This indicates a need for urgent support at that time.

insights.title: Consistent Target Practice Needed
insights.description: Despite passing in Assessments 2 and 3, your scores remain below the target threshold of 70, indicating that further practice is necessary to reach your full potential.

educational_implications: Your improvement suggests that with the right support and practice, you can enhance your understanding of the material.
The need for targeted practice indicates that while you are passing, there is still room for growth to reach your target performance.

recommendations.action: Review Assessment 1 with tutor support to focus on missed core concepts.
recommendations.rationale: This will help solidify your understanding and prevent similar struggles in future assessments.

recommendations.action: Engage in targeted practice on topics related to Assessments 2 and 3.
recommendations.rationale: This will help you move from pass-level to target-level performance.

confidence.reason: The data quality is strong with a large sample size of 649 students across 3 assessments.

v3_warnings: Task-aware V3 prompt exceeded the configured soft token ratio (2.6472 > 1.2).
Task-aware V3 prompt remained above the experimental safety ratio after optional evidence trimming; raw rows were preserved.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify the observed score trend direction.
Identify assessments below returned pass or target thresholds.

required_supporting_outputs.description: Provide recommended_action for the weakest recent assessment only when that field is present.

evaluation_constraints.description: If fewer than 3 assessment data points are available, state that evidence is insufficient for a reliable trend rather than asserting a stable direction.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
